prompt: Single-frame, magazine-cover-quality conceptual data visualization (not a standard chart), photoreal-cinematic editorial style. Scene: a vast banquet table in a dim hall, viewed from slightly above and centered perspective. Exactly 100 place settings represented as human silhouettes seated at the table. The nearest 10 seats (top 10%) occupy over half of the total table area and are overloaded with extravagant food towers, gold cutlery, bright warm light, and visual abundance. The remaining 90 seats are compressed into the far end; among them, the nearest 40 have modest plain bread and water, and the farthest 50 are almost in darkness with mostly empty plates and a few crumbs. Keep this as one continuous table in a single frame, no split panels, no axes, no labels, no legends, no text overlays. The image must communicate the ratio emotionally through space, scale, and light: concentration of income at one end, scarcity for the majority at the other. Color script: rich amber and deep crimson in the affluent zone, desaturated blue-gray in the deprived zone, with a smooth gradient transition. Add subtle atmospheric dust and long shadows for gravity. Composition should feel intentional, non-generic, and immediately interpretable without reading. High detail, 4k look, strong figure-ground contrast. Negative prompt: no bar chart, no line chart, no pie chart, no scatter plot, no infographic icons, no dashboard UI, no globe, no thermometer, no text, no caption, no watermark, no collage, no multi-panel layout.
dataset_name: World Inequality Database (WID): Global income shares by population groups
insight: Global income is highly concentrated: the top 10% receive over half of all income, while the bottom half receives only a small share.
